title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Chief Constable Trotter visits Jules and Molly Davis to warn them of imminent danger following the murder of Madame Gregg. Trotter explains that in 1940, three refugee children took shelter at Longridge Farm, where one child died due to neglect and abuse. The Greggs were imprisoned for the incident; Mr. Gregg died during an escape attempt, and Madame Gregg was recently released before being murdered.

Trotter reveals that a notebook found near the crime scene contains two addresses: 74 Carlisle Street, where the murder occurred, and Monkswell Manor, the Davises' location. The notebook is titled "Three Blind Mice" and includes a drawing of three mice and the nursery rhyme's sheet music. A note pinned to the victim's body stated, "This is the first one." Trotter notes that of the three children from the 1940 case, one girl was adopted and one boy, now twenty-three, was a deserting soldier described by a psychiatrist as "not right."

Trotter, acting on behalf of Inspector Hogben, intends to investigate the residents of Monkswell Manor to find a connection to the Longridge Farm case. The residents include Mrs. Poyle, Colonel Metcalf, Mr. Christopher Lane, and Mr. Paravicini. Jules suggests the killer might be Christopher Rene, but Trotter warns that the murderer could already be inside the house, as all guests arrived after the murder. Trotter also notes that a description of the suspect—a lean person in a black coat and light hat—could apply to several people in the house.
